briefdescription.para: Get a devicetree property value.

detaileddescription.para: For properties whose bindings have the following types, this macro expands to:

listitem.para: string: a string literal
boolean: 0 if the property is false, or 1 if it is true
int: the property's value as an integer literal
array, uint8-array, string-array: an initializer expression in braces, whose elements are integer or string literals (like {0, 1, 2}, {"hello", "world"}, etc.)
phandle: a node identifier for the node with that phandle

detaileddescription.para: A property's type is usually defined by its binding. In some special cases, it has an assumed type defined by the devicetree specification even when no binding is available: compatible has type string-array, status has type string, and interrupt-controller has type boolean.
For other properties or properties with unknown type due to a missing binding, behavior is undefined.
For usage examples, see DT_PATH(), DT_ALIAS(), DT_NODELABEL(), and DT_INST() above.

simplesect.para: a representation of the property's value

briefdescription.para: Get a property's logical length.

detaileddescription.para: Here, "length" is a number of elements, which may differ from the property's size in bytes.
The return value depends on the property's type:

listitem.para: for types array, string-array, and uint8-array, this expands to the number of elements in the array
for type phandles, this expands to the number of phandles
for type phandle-array, this expands to the number of phandle and specifier blocks in the property
for type phandle, this expands to 1 (so that a phandle can be treated as a degenerate case of phandles with length 1)
for type string, this expands to 1 (so that a string can be treated as a degenerate case of string-array with length 1)

detaileddescription.para: These properties are handled as special cases:

listitem.para: reg property: use DT_NUM_REGS(node_id) instead
interrupts property: use DT_NUM_IRQS(node_id) instead

detaileddescription.para: It is an error to use this macro with the ranges, dma-ranges, reg or interrupts properties.
For other properties, behavior is undefined.

parameterdescription.para: a lowercase-and-underscores property with a logical length

simplesect.para: the property's length

briefdescription.para: Like DT_PROP_LEN(), but with a fallback to default_value.

detaileddescription.para: If the property is defined (as determined by DT_NODE_HAS_PROP()), this expands to DT_PROP_LEN(node_id, prop). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case.
Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a lowercase-and-underscores property with a logical length
a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the property's length or the given default value

briefdescription.para: Is index idx valid for an array type property?

detaileddescription.para: If this returns 1, then DT_PROP_BY_IDX(node_id, prop, idx) or DT_PHA_BY_IDX(node_id, prop, idx, ...) are valid at index idx. If it returns 0, it is an error to use those macros with that index.
These properties are handled as special cases:

listitem.para: reg property: use DT_REG_HAS_IDX(node_id, idx) instead
interrupts property: use DT_IRQ_HAS_IDX(node_id, idx) instead

detaileddescription.para: It is an error to use this macro with the reg or interrupts properties.

parameterdescription.para: a lowercase-and-underscores property with a logical length

simplesect.para: An expression which evaluates to 1 if idx is a valid index into the given property, and 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Is name name available in a foo-names property?

detaileddescription.para: This property is handled as special case:

listitem.para: interrupts property: use DT_IRQ_HAS_NAME(node_id, idx) instead

detaileddescription.para: It is an error to use this macro with the interrupts property.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example usage:

simplesect.para: An expression which evaluates to 1 if "name" is an available name into the given property, and 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the value at index idx in an array type property.

detaileddescription.para: It might help to read the argument order as being similar to node->property[index].
The return value depends on the property's type:

listitem.para: for types array, string-array, uint8-array, and phandles, this expands to the idx-th array element as an integer, string literal, integer, and node identifier respectively
for type phandle, idx must be 0 and the expansion is a node identifier (this treats phandle like a phandles of length 1)
for type string, idx must be 0 and the expansion is the entire string (this treats string like string-array of length 1)

detaileddescription.para: These properties are handled as special cases:

listitem.para: reg: use DT_REG_ADDR_BY_IDX() or DT_REG_SIZE_BY_IDX() instead
interrupts: use DT_IRQ_BY_IDX()
ranges: use DT_NUM_RANGES()
dma-ranges: it is an error to use this property with DT_PROP_BY_IDX()

detaileddescription.para: For properties of other types, behavior is undefined.

simplesect.para: a representation of the idx-th element of the property

briefdescription.para: Get the last element of an array type property.

simplesect.para: a representation of the last element of the property

briefdescription.para: Like DT_PROP(), but with a fallback to default_value.

detaileddescription.para: If the value exists, this expands to DT_PROP(node_id, prop). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case.
Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the property's value or default_value

briefdescription.para: Get a property array value's index into its enumeration values.

detaileddescription.para: The return values start at zero.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example bindings fragment:
Example usage:

simplesect.para: zero-based index of the property's value in its enum: list

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to DT_ENUM_IDX_BY_IDX(node_id, prop, 0).

simplesect.para: zero-based index of the property's value in its enum: list

briefdescription.para: Like DT_ENUM_IDX_BY_IDX(), but with a fallback to a default enum index.

detaileddescription.para: If the value exists, this expands to its zero based index value thanks to DT_ENUM_IDX_BY_IDX(node_id, prop, idx).
Otherwise, this expands to provided default index enum value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback index value to expand to

simplesect.para: zero-based index of the property's value in its enum if present, default_idx_value otherwise

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to DT_ENUM_IDX_BY_IDX_OR(node_id, prop, 0, default_idx_value).

parameterdescription.para: a fallback index value to expand to

simplesect.para: zero-based index of the property's value in its enum if present, default_idx_value otherwise

briefdescription.para: Does a node enumeration property array have a given value?

simplesect.para: 1 if the node property has the value value, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Does a node enumeration property have a given value?

simplesect.para: 1 if the node property has the value value, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get a string property's value as a token.

detaileddescription.para: This removes "the quotes" from a string property's value, converting any non-alphanumeric characters to underscores. This can be useful, for example, when programmatically using the value to form a C variable or code.
DT_STRING_TOKEN() can only be used for properties with string type.
It is an error to use DT_STRING_TOKEN() in other circumstances.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example bindings fragment:
Example usage:
Notice how:

listitem.para: Unlike C identifiers, the property values may begin with a number. It's the user's responsibility not to use such values as the name of a C identifier.
The uppercased "FOO" in the DTS remains FOO as a token. It is not converted to foo.
The whitespace in the DTS "123 foo" string is converted to 123_foo as a token.

simplesect.para: the value of prop as a token, i.e. without any quotes and with special characters converted to underscores

briefdescription.para: Like DT_STRING_TOKEN(), but with a fallback to default_value.

detaileddescription.para: If the value exists, this expands to DT_STRING_TOKEN(node_id, prop). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case.
Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the property's value as a token, or default_value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_STRING_TOKEN(), but uppercased.

detaileddescription.para: This removes "the quotes" from a string property's value, converting any non-alphanumeric characters to underscores, and capitalizing the result. This can be useful, for example, when programmatically using the value to form a C variable or code.
DT_STRING_UPPER_TOKEN() can only be used for properties with string type.
It is an error to use DT_STRING_UPPER_TOKEN() in other circumstances.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example bindings fragment:
Example usage:
Notice how:

listitem.para: Unlike C identifiers, the property values may begin with a number. It's the user's responsibility not to use such values as the name of a C identifier.
The lowercased "foo" in the DTS becomes FOO as a token, i.e. it is uppercased.
The whitespace in the DTS "123 foo" string is converted to 123_FOO as a token, i.e. it is uppercased and whitespace becomes an underscore.

simplesect.para: the value of prop as an uppercased token, i.e. without any quotes and with special characters converted to underscores

briefdescription.para: Like DT_STRING_UPPER_TOKEN(), but with a fallback to default_value.

detaileddescription.para: If the value exists, this expands to DT_STRING_UPPER_TOKEN(node_id, prop). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case.
Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the property's value as an uppercased token, or default_value

briefdescription.para: Get a string property's value as an unquoted sequence of tokens.

detaileddescription.para: This removes "the quotes" from string-valued properties. That can be useful, for example, when defining floating point values as a string in devicetree that you would like to use to initialize a float or double variable in C.
DT_STRING_UNQUOTED() can only be used for properties with string type.
It is an error to use DT_STRING_UNQUOTED() in other circumstances.

briefdescription.para: Like DT_STRING_UNQUOTED(), but with a fallback to default_value.

detaileddescription.para: If the value exists, this expands to DT_STRING_UNQUOTED(node_id, prop). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case.
Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the property's value as a sequence of tokens, with no quotes, or default_value

briefdescription.para: Get an element out of a string-array property as a token.

detaileddescription.para: This removes "the quotes" from an element in the array, and converts non-alphanumeric characters to underscores. That can be useful, for example, when programmatically using the value to form a C variable or code.
DT_STRING_TOKEN_BY_IDX() can only be used for properties with string-array type.
It is an error to use DT_STRING_TOKEN_BY_IDX() in other circumstances.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example bindings fragment:
Example usage:
For more information, see DT_STRING_TOKEN.

simplesect.para: the element in prop at index idx as a token

briefdescription.para: Like DT_STRING_TOKEN_BY_IDX(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the element in prop at index idx as a token, or default_value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_STRING_TOKEN_BY_IDX(), but uppercased.

detaileddescription.para: This removes "the quotes" and capitalizes an element in the array, and converts non-alphanumeric characters to underscores. That can be useful, for example, when programmatically using the value to form a C variable or code.
DT_STRING_UPPER_TOKEN_BY_IDX() can only be used for properties with string-array type.
It is an error to use DT_STRING_UPPER_TOKEN_BY_IDX() in other circumstances.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example bindings fragment:
Example usage:
For more information, see DT_STRING_UPPER_TOKEN.

simplesect.para: the element in prop at index idx as an uppercased token

briefdescription.para: Get a string array item value as an unquoted sequence of tokens.

detaileddescription.para: This removes "the quotes" from string-valued item. That can be useful, for example, when defining floating point values as a string in devicetree that you would like to use to initialize a float or double variable in C.
DT_STRING_UNQUOTED_BY_IDX() can only be used for properties with string-array type.
It is an error to use DT_STRING_UNQUOTED_BY_IDX() in other circumstances.

briefdescription.para: Get a property value from a phandle in a property.

detaileddescription.para: This is a shorthand for:
That is, prop is a property of the phandle's node, not a property of node_id.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example usage:

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores property with type phandle, phandles, or phandle-array
logical index into phs, which must be zero if phs has type phandle
lowercase-and-underscores property of the phandle's node

simplesect.para: the property's value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_PROP_BY_PHANDLE_IDX(), but with a fallback to default_value.

detaileddescription.para: If the value exists, this expands to DT_PROP_BY_PHANDLE_IDX(node_id, phs, idx, prop). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case.
Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores property with type phandle, phandles, or phandle-array
logical index into phs, which must be zero if phs has type phandle
lowercase-and-underscores property of the phandle's node
a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the property's value

briefdescription.para: Get a property value from a phandle's node.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to DT_PROP_BY_PHANDLE_IDX(node_id, ph, 0, prop).

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores property of node_id with type phandle
lowercase-and-underscores property of the phandle's node

simplesect.para: the property's value

briefdescription.para: Get a phandle-array specifier cell value at an index.

detaileddescription.para: It might help to read the argument order as being similar to node->phandle_array[index].cell. That is, the cell value is in the pha property of node_id, inside the specifier at index idx.
Example devicetree fragment:
Bindings fragment for the gpio0 and gpio1 nodes:
Above, gpios has two elements:

listitem.para: index 0 has specifier <17 0x1>, so its pin cell is 17, and its flags cell is 0x1
index 1 has specifier <5 0x3>, so pin is 5 and flags is 0x3

detaileddescription.para: Example usage:

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores cell name within the specifier at pha index idx

simplesect.para: the cell's value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_PHA_BY_IDX(), but with a fallback to default_value.

detaileddescription.para: If the value exists, this expands to DT_PHA_BY_IDX(node_id, pha, idx, cell). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case.
Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores cell name within the specifier at pha index idx
a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the cell's value or default_value

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to DT_PHA_BY_IDX(node_id, pha, 0, cell)

simplesect.para: the cell's value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_PHA(), but with a fallback to default_value.

detaileddescription.para: If the value exists, this expands to DT_PHA(node_id, pha, cell). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case.
Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the cell's value or default_value

briefdescription.para: Get a value within a phandle-array specifier by name.

detaileddescription.para: This is like DT_PHA_BY_IDX(), except it treats pha as a structure where each array element has a name.

It might help to read the argument order as being similar to node->phandle_struct.name.cell. That is, the cell value is in the pha property of node_id, treated as a data structure where each array element has a name.
Example devicetree fragment:
Bindings fragment for the "adc1" and "adc2" nodes:
Example usage:

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores name of a specifier in pha
lowercase-and-underscores cell name in the named specifier

simplesect.para: the cell's value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_PHA_BY_NAME(), but with a fallback to default_value.

detaileddescription.para: If the value exists, this expands to DT_PHA_BY_NAME(node_id, pha, name, cell). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case.
Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores name of a specifier in pha
lowercase-and-underscores cell name in the named specifier
a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the cell's value or default_value

briefdescription.para: Get a phandle's node identifier from a phandle array by name.

detaileddescription.para: It might help to read the argument order as being similar to node->phandle_struct.name.phandle. That is, the phandle array is treated as a structure with named elements. The return value is the node identifier for a phandle inside the structure.
Example devicetree fragment:
Above, "io-channels" has two elements:

listitem.para: the element named "SENSOR" has phandle &adc1
the element named "BANDGAP" has phandle &adc2

detaileddescription.para: Example usage:
Notice how devicetree properties and names are lowercased, and non-alphanumeric characters are converted to underscores.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores name of an element in pha

simplesect.para: a node identifier for the node with that phandle

briefdescription.para: Get a node identifier for a phandle in a property.

detaileddescription.para: When a node's value at a logical index contains a phandle, this macro returns a node identifier for the node with that phandle.
Therefore, if prop has type phandle, idx must be zero. (A phandle type is treated as a phandles with a fixed length of 1).
Example devicetree fragment:
Above, foo has type phandles and has two elements:

listitem.para: index 0 has phandle &n2, which is node-2's phandle
index 1 has phandle &n3, which is node-3's phandle

detaileddescription.para: Example usage:
Behavior is analogous for phandle-arrays.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores property name in node_id with type phandle, phandles or phandle-array

simplesect.para: node identifier for the node with the phandle at that index

briefdescription.para: Get a node identifier for a phandle property's value.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to DT_PHANDLE_BY_IDX(node_id, prop, 0). Its primary benefit is readability when prop has type phandle.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores property of node_id with type phandle

simplesect.para: a node identifier for the node pointed to by "ph"
